summary: Russia launched a massive combined strike on 24‑25 May employing Iskander, Kinzhal, Zircon, cruise missiles and hundreds of Shahed drones, heavily targeting Kyiv and other regions. Ukraine reported significant civilian casualties in Kyiv and Bila Tserkva, while also striking Russian oil infrastructure in the Black Sea region. Preparations for a new "Oreshnik" hypersonic missile attack are underway, heightening the risk of further high‑value strikes.
key_points: Russia deployed 55 missiles and 549 drones, including Iskander, Kinzhal, Zircon and Shahed UAVs, in a coordinated strike on Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Kyiv suffered over 40 hit locations, one civilian death and 21 injuries, with metro and residential damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
Ukrainian forces struck the Russian Tamanneftegaz terminal in the Black Sea, damaging a key oil export hub (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
Russia prepared to use the new hypersonic "Oreshnik" missile against Ukrainian targets, cited by President Zelensky (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
An "Oreshnik" missile hit a garage cooperative in Bila Tserkva, causing structural damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81413).
A cruise missile flew over Nizhyn district heading toward Kyiv, indicating continued long‑range missile threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
UAVs were observed over the Kyiv reservoir moving toward the capital, posing immediate air‑defence challenges (Source: @kpszsu/62641).
A Russian drone strike hit the Albanian embassy residential complex, raising diplomatic security concerns (Source: @UkraineNow/81428).

sections.executive_summary: Russia unleashed a massive combined missile and drone barrage, including Iskander, Kinzhal, Zircon and hundreds of Shahed UAVs, heavily striking Kyiv and causing civilian casualties. Ukraine responded with a strike on the Russian Tamanneftegaz oil terminal, while preparing for possible "Oreshnik" hypersonic attacks. Diplomatic sites and critical infrastructure remain under heightened threat across multiple fronts.

points: Ukrainian air defences downed 55 missiles and 549 hostile UAVs during the 24‑25 May combined attack, preventing further damage to key cities (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
In Kyiv, over 40 locations were hit, resulting in one civilian death, 21 injuries, and damage to the Lukianivska metro station and residential blocks (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
Ukrainian forces struck the Russian Tamanneftegaz terminal in the Black Sea region, damaging a major oil‑export facility that handles up to 20 Mt annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
Ukrainian intelligence reported preparations for a Russian "Oreshnik" hypersonic strike, prompting heightened civil‑defence alerts (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
A cruise missile over Nizhyn district was tracked heading toward Kyiv, indicating continued long‑range missile threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
UAVs were observed over the Kyiv water reservoir moving toward the capital, forcing rapid mobilisation of SAM units (Source: @kpszsu/62641).
A Russian drone strike damaged the residential complex housing the Albanian ambassador, raising diplomatic security concerns (Source: @UkraineNow/81428).

Russia launched 55 missiles, including 11 Iskander‑M/С‑400 and 44 cruise missiles (X‑101/Iskander‑K/Caliber), from launch sites in Astrakhan, Bryansk, Crimea and the Black Sea (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Two aerobalistic Kinzhal missiles were fired from Lipetsk region, targeting Ukrainian command‑and‑control nodes (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Three Zircon anti‑ship missiles were launched from Crimea and Kursk region, aimed at Ukrainian coastal assets (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Approximately 600 Shahed‑type loitering munitions were released from multiple Russian oblasts, creating dense UAV swarms over Kyiv and eastern regions (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Guided aerial bombs were dropped on Kharkiv and Donetsk oblasts, indicating intensified tactical air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/62619).
Russia claimed the "Oreshnik" hypersonic missile was used against Bila Tserkva, though details remain sparse (Source: @UkraineNow/81413).
Russian forces reported no planned strikes on Ukrainian civilian infrastructure in the latest statements, contradicting observed attacks (Source: @tass_agency/377305).
Shahed UAVs were reported over Sumy north‑west, heading toward Romny, indicating pressure on supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/62636).
UAVs from Chernihiv region moved toward Kyiv and Ivankiv, threatening northern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/62650).
Russian missile launches from Volgograd and Kursk regions targeted eastern Ukrainian command posts, using X‑101 cruise missiles (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Guided aerial bomb strikes hit Kharkiv and Donetsk oblasts, causing infrastructure damage (Source: @kpszsu/62619).
Ukrainian forces reported successful interception of multiple drones over the eastern front, limiting enemy reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
UAVs detected over the Kyiv water reservoir moving southward, prompting SAM activation (Source: @kpszsu/62641).
Cruise missile over Nizhyn district headed for Kyiv, suggesting a northern trajectory for long‑range strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Russian launch sites in Bryansk fired Iskander missiles toward northern Ukrainian targets (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defence reported downing of several Shahed drones over Poltava and Cherkasy regions, protecting northern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
UAV activity observed over Chernihiv heading to Ivankiv, indicating continued pressure on the northern corridor (Source: @kpszsu/62650).
Ukrainian strike on the Tamanneftegaz terminal in the Black Sea disrupted Russian oil export capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
Russian missile launches from Crimea targeted southern Ukrainian coastal installations with Zircon missiles (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
UAV swarms from the Black Sea region were intercepted over Odesa, limiting Russian reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian forces reported no civilian infrastructure strikes in the south, despite ongoing missile activity (Source: @tass_agency/377305).
Ukrainian units reported increased artillery engagements near the front line in Kherson, aiming to degrade Russian logistics (Source: @kpszsu/62653).
Ukraine's air defence shot down 55 missiles and 549 UAVs in a single day, the highest recorded tally (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Shahed loitering munitions were the predominant UAV type, with over 600 launched from multiple Russian oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian aerobalistic Kinzhal missiles were used for the first time against Ukrainian command sites (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian forces employed guided aerial bombs on Kharkiv and Donetsk, indicating expanded use of precision air‑to‑ground weapons (Source: @kpszsu/62619).
UAVs over Kyiv reservoir and Nizhyn district highlight persistent low‑altitude threats requiring layered defence (Source: @kpszsu/62641, @mon1tor_ua/67199).

key_developments: Russia executed a massive combined strike using 55 missiles—including Iskander, Kinzhal and Zircon—and 600 Shahed drones, overwhelming Ukrainian air defences (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defences intercepted 55 missiles and 549 UAVs, marking the highest single‑day interception count (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Kyiv suffered over 40 hit locations, one civilian death and 21 injuries, with damage to metro infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
Ukrainian forces struck the Russian Tamanneftegaz oil terminal in the Black Sea, disrupting a key export hub (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
President Zelensky warned of imminent Russian "Oreshnik" hypersonic missile preparations, raising strategic alarm (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
An "Oreshnik" missile hit a garage cooperative in Bila Tserkva, confirming use of the new weapon (Source: @UkraineNow/81413).
A cruise missile over Nizhyn district headed toward Kyiv, indicating continued long‑range missile threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are escalating their strike capability by integrating hypersonic "Oreshnik" missiles, advanced cruise missiles and dense Shahed UAV swarms, targeting both strategic command centres and civilian infrastructure. Ukrainian air defences have demonstrated resilience but face saturation risks, especially over the capital and northern corridors. Continued Ukrainian counter‑strikes on Russian energy assets suggest a reciprocal escalation in targeting critical logistics.
sections.regional_response: Western partners have heightened intelligence sharing, as evidenced by Ukrainian reports of American and European data on the "Oreshnik" preparation. NATO allies are likely to increase air‑defence aid, while diplomatic pressure mounts after attacks on foreign embassy residences. Russia's claims of limited civilian targeting contrast with observed strikes, potentially affecting its international narrative.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of launch sites in Astrakhan, Bryansk and Crimea is critical for early warning of hypersonic and cruise missile launches. UAV traffic from Russian oblasts to Kyiv and the north requires enhanced radar coverage. The effectiveness of Ukrainian strikes on Russian oil infrastructure may prompt retaliatory attacks on energy facilities in occupied territories.
